description: Common pathname manipulations for POSIX and Windows paths.

functions.name: join

params.name: *paths
params.description: Additional path components to join. If a component is absolute, all previous components are discarded.

returns.description: Concatenation of path components with the directory separator.

functions.description: Join one or more path components, inserting the OS separator as needed. If a component is an absolute path, all previous components are discarded.

functions.name: split

returns.description: A pair (head, tail) where tail is the last pathname component and head is everything before it.

functions.description: Split a pathname into (head, tail) where tail is the last component. Equivalent to (dirname(path), basename(path)).

functions.name: splitext

returns.description: A pair (root, ext) where ext begins with a period and contains at most one period.

functions.description: Split a pathname into a pair (root, ext) such that root + ext == path. The extension includes the leading period.

functions.name: basename

returns.description: The final component of the path.

functions.description: Return the final component of a pathname. Equivalent to split(path)[1].

functions.name: dirname

returns.description: The directory component of the path.

functions.description: Return the directory component of a pathname. Equivalent to split(path)[0].

functions.name: exists

returns.description: True if path exists, False otherwise.

functions.description: Return True if path refers to an existing path or file descriptor. Returns False for broken symbolic links.

functions.name: isfile

returns.description: True if path is an existing regular file.

functions.description: Return True if path is an existing regular file. Follows symbolic links.

functions.name: isdir

returns.description: True if path is an existing directory.

functions.description: Return True if path is an existing directory. Follows symbolic links.

functions.name: isabs

returns.description: True if path is an absolute pathname.

functions.description: Return True if path is an absolute pathname. On Unix, that means it begins with a slash; on Windows, it begins with a drive letter and backslash.

functions.name: islink

returns.description: True if path is a symbolic link.

functions.description: Return True if path refers to a directory entry that is a symbolic link. Always False on platforms that do not support symbolic links.

functions.name: getsize

returns.description: Size in bytes.

functions.description: Return the size, in bytes, of path. Raises os.error if the file does not exist or is inaccessible.

functions.name: getmtime

returns.description: Time of last modification as seconds since the epoch.

functions.description: Return the time of last modification of path. The return value is a number giving the number of seconds since the epoch.

functions.name: getatime

returns.description: Time of last access as seconds since the epoch.

functions.description: Return the time of last access of path. The return value is a number giving the number of seconds since the epoch.

functions.name: getctime

returns.description: Time of creation (Windows) or last metadata change (Unix) as seconds since the epoch.

functions.description: Return the system's ctime, which on Unix is the time of the last metadata change, and on Windows is the creation time of path.

functions.name: abspath

returns.description: Absolute version of the path.

functions.description: Return a normalized absolutized version of the pathname path. Equivalent to normpath(join(os.getcwd(), path)).

functions.name: realpath

returns.description: Canonical path with symbolic links resolved.

functions.description: Return the canonical path of the specified filename, eliminating any symbolic links encountered in the path.

functions.name: normpath

returns.description: Normalized path.

functions.description: Normalize a pathname by collapsing redundant separators and up-level references (A//B, A/./B, A/foo/../B all become A/B).

functions.name: normcase

returns.description: Case-normalized path.

functions.description: Normalize the case of a pathname. On Windows, convert all characters to lowercase and forward slashes to backslashes. On Unix, return the path unchanged.

functions.name: expanduser

returns.description: Path with ~ replaced by the user's home directory.

functions.description: Expand ~ and ~user constructs in the path. If the expansion fails or the path does not begin with ~, the path is returned unchanged.

functions.name: expandvars

params.name: path
params.description: Path containing environment variables to expand.

returns.description: Path with environment variables expanded.

functions.description: Expand shell variables of the form $name and ${name} in the path. Undefined variables are left unchanged.

functions.name: commonprefix

returns.description: Longest common leading component (character by character, not path-aware).

functions.description: Return the longest path prefix (taken character by character) that is a prefix of all paths in list. Note: this operates character-by-character and may return an invalid path.

functions.name: relpath

params.name: start
params.description: Starting directory for computing the relative path. Defaults to os.curdir.

returns.description: A relative filepath to path from the start directory.

functions.description: Return a relative filepath to path either from the current directory or from an optional start directory.

functions.name: samefile

returns.description: True if both pathnames refer to the same file or directory.

functions.description: Return True if both pathname arguments refer to the same file or directory (as indicated by device number and inode number). Raises an exception if stat() fails on either path. Unix only.

functions.name: walk

params.name: visit
params.description: Function called with (arg, dirname, names) for each directory.

params.name: arg
params.description: Argument passed to the visit function.

returns.description: None.

functions.description: Directory tree walk with callback function. Deprecated since Python 2.6; use os.walk() instead.
